svg.aria-label: What is hand-curated versus computed: editorial fields versus derived views, and the at-bar gate that a tile must pass before it may say live
svg.text: What is curated, what is computed, and the gate for "live"
the tile you click carries two kinds of fields; only the editorial ones are typed by hand, and the status field is governed.
EDITORIAL · hand-curated in registry.json
· name, oneliner, stage, solutionType
· status (planned / building / live), governed, see below
· featured flag, catalog order
· links.app / links.repo / links.docs
· dataVerdict grade (GREEN / YELLOW / RED) + note
a human writes these; they are claims the author stands behind
and are reviewed, never machine-invented.
DERIVED · computed at load by catalog.ts
· hero counts (total / live / building / planned)
· the value-chain lanes (group by stage, in order)
· the coverage matrix (stage x facet counts)
· the featured row, the facet + status filters
no hand-kept totals: every number recomputes from the rows,
so the counts can never drift from the catalog.
The at-bar gate on status: live
deploying an app is NOT enough to call it live.
planned: no repo/URL yet; a roadmap tile
building: deployed and openable ("Try"),
but NOT yet through its at-bar review
live: passed the RotorVitals quality bar,
real data lane · full method ladder · deep docs · honest claims
so a "live" dot is a promise the app was audited, not merely that
it responds to a URL. This is the hub's one editorial guarantee.
